prevalence.source: ECDC Tuberculosis Surveillance / WHO Regional Office for Europe
prevalence.trend: decreasing overall; higher burden persists in some Eastern EU countries and vulnerable groups

burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / WHO Europe

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Microbiological confirmation (NAAT, smear/culture) with drug susceptibility testing; radiology supportive; ECDC/WHO Europe surveillance definitions

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: contact tracing and targeted screening in high-risk groups; coverage varies by member state
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~80-85% treatment success for drug-susceptible TB; lower for MDR-TB

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Broad access to molecular diagnostics and DST; standardized reporting to ECDC in most member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Standard HRZE-based regimens for drug-susceptible TB; MDR-TB managed with specialist teams and newer agents (e.g., bedaquiline/linezolid) per national/WHO guidance
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Persistent MDR-TB in parts of Eastern EU, care continuity for migrants, and social vulnerability barriers to adherence
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong surveillance systems, high laboratory capacity for DST, and structured contact tracing and public health follow-up
